name: What proof do I need if I slipped on a wet floor at work with no witnesses?
description: Photos, report, medical docs—even without witnesses, comp covers you.[2]

mainEntity.name: What proof do I need if I slipped on a wet floor at work with no witnesses?

acceptedAnswer.text: Photos, report, medical docs—even without witnesses, comp covers you.[2]
acceptedAnswer.description: **Photos of the hazard, incident report, medical records, and your detailed statement suffice initially.** Security footage or coworker recollections strengthen it; workers' comp doesn't require fault proof.[1][2] Timestamp everything and note prior complaints about the area. If denied, expert testimony on unsafe conditions helps. Preserve shoes/clothes if relevant. A lawyer gathers and presents evidence effectively.
